title: Session Timeout Enforcement on Payment Web Admin Consoles

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 8.2.8 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 8.2.8 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.55: Session Timeout Enforcement on Payment Web Admin Consoles.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.55' running on index=web sourcetype="access_combined" for /admin URIs with session_id, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=web sourcetype="access_combined" earliest=-24h
| where match(uri,"(?i)/admin|/portal/manage")
| transaction session_id maxpause=30m maxspan=8h
| eval idle_mins=round(duration/60,2)
| where idle_mins>15
| stats max(idle_mins) as max_idle by session_id, clientip
| sort - max_idle
description: Measures idle session duration before logout on administrative payment portals using HTTP session cookies or app logs so inactive sessions re-authenticate within policy limits.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes web and sourcetypes access_combined. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Align `maxpause` with configured server session timeout. (2) Mask `session_id` in dashboards. (3) Tune for long-running report downloads. (4) Pair with WAF session fixation checks. Indexes required: `index=web`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=access_combined`.
visualization: Histogram (idle_mins), table (longest sessions), single value (sessions exceeding policy).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.Intrusion_Detection by Intrusion_Detection.signature, Intrusion_Detection.dest, Intrusion_Detection.severity | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: CDE access from authorized payment ops, scheduled batch reconciliation, or PCI assessor activities.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Stream (1809), `index=web` `access_combined`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=web` `sourcetype="access_combined"` for `/admin` URIs with `session_id`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Align `maxpause` with configured server session timeout. (2) Mask `session_id` in dashboards. (3) Tune for long-running report downloads. (4) Pair with WAF session fixation checks.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=web sourcetype="access_combined" earliest=-24h
| where match(uri,"(?i)/admin|/portal/manage")
| transaction session_id maxpause=30m maxspan=8h
| eval idle_mins=round(duration/60,2)
| where idle_mins>15
| stats max(idle_mins) as max_idle by session_id, clientip
| sort - max_idle
```

Understanding this SPL

**Session Timeout Enforcement on Payment Web Admin Consoles (PCI DSS Req 8.2.8)** — Measures idle session duration before logout on administrative payment portals using HTTP session cookies or app logs so inactive sessions re-authenticate within policy limits.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=web` `sourcetype="access_combined"` for `/admin` URIs with `session_id`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Stream (1809), `index=web` `access_combined`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: web; **sourcetype**: access_combined. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=web, sourcetype="access_combined", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(uri,"(?i)/admin|/portal/manage")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Groups related events into transactions — prefer `maxspan`/`maxpause`/`maxevents` for bounded memory.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **idle_mins** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where idle_mins>15` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by session_id, clientip** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Histogram (idle_mins), table (longest sessions), single value (sessions exceeding policy).
grandmaExplanation: We measures idle session duration before logout on administrative payment portals using HTTP session cookies or app logs so inactive sessions re-authenticate within policy limits. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Intrusion_Detection (CIM data model).
